text.title: Kubernetes Cost Optimisation Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real finops-devops-cost-kubernetes deployments, each paired with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: Cluster sits at 25% CPU / 40% memory utilisation despite frequent autoscaling.
antipattern.root-cause: Single uniform node pool forces autoscaler to size for the worst-case pod.
antipattern.fix: Split pools by workload class; taint Spot + GPU pools; size pool defaults to median pod shape.

antipattern.root-cause: Containers missing requests / limits; scheduler can't pack honestly.
antipattern.fix: Set requests = P95 observed; memory request = memory limit; admission controller enforces both.

antipattern.symptom: Pod count lags traffic spikes; users see 503 before scale-out completes.
antipattern.root-cause: HPA tied to CPU only; CPU saturates after the user impact.
antipattern.fix: Add a business metric (RPS or queue depth) via Custom or External metric and lower scale-out latency.

antipattern.symptom: Cluster upgrade or node drain takes the whole service down for the maintenance window.
antipattern.root-cause: Deployment has > 1 replicas but no PodDisruptionBudget.
antipattern.fix: Define a PDB with minAvailable ≥ 1; raise replica count if PDB starves voluntary disruption.
